chapter: Chapter: The Amazing World of Solutes, Solvents, and Solutions

questions.question: Why does stirring speed up the process of dissolving?

options.B: It increases contact between solute and solvent particles

questions.explanation: Stirring brings fresh solvent particles into contact with the solute, speeding dissolution.

questions.question: Which condition will produce the fastest dissolving of sugar in water?
questions.explanation: Higher temperature and stirring both increase the rate of dissolving.

questions.question: Why do cold drinks lose their fizz faster when kept in warm places?
questions.explanation: Higher temperature reduces gas solubility, causing carbon dioxide to escape.

questions.question: A solid object sinks in water. Which statement must be true?

options.A: Its density is lower than water
options.B: Its density is equal to water
options.C: Its density is greater than water

questions.explanation: Objects sink when their density is greater than the density of water.

questions.question: Why do ships made of steel float on water?

options.B: Ships contain hollow spaces reducing overall density

questions.explanation: The hollow structure lowers the ship's average density.

questions.question: What will happen to density if volume increases but mass remains constant?
questions.explanation: Density = Mass ÷ Volume, so increasing volume decreases density.

questions.question: Why does hot air rise in the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Heating expands air, decreasing density and causing it to rise.

questions.question: If salt is added to water, what happens to the density of water?
questions.explanation: Adding salt increases the mass without significantly increasing volume.

questions.question: Which property allows scientists to identify an unknown liquid accurately?
questions.explanation: Density is a characteristic property unique to substances.

questions.question: What is the most suitable method to measure volume of an irregular stone?
questions.explanation: Water displacement measures irregular volumes accurately.

questions.question: Why must readings of measuring cylinders be taken at eye level?
questions.explanation: Eye-level reading prevents measurement errors.

questions.question: Which condition increases density of a gas?
questions.explanation: Higher pressure compresses gas particles increasing density.

questions.question: Which factor mainly determines whether a substance floats?
questions.explanation: Floating depends on density relative to the liquid.

questions.question: Why does ice float though it is made of water?
questions.explanation: Water expands during freezing lowering density.

questions.question: Which situation shows the application of density principle?
questions.explanation: Ship flotation depends on density differences.

questions.question: Which factor does NOT affect the rate of dissolving?
questions.explanation: Colour does not influence dissolving rate.

questions.question: Which substance would float highest in salt water?
questions.explanation: Wood has much lower density than salt water.

questions.question: Why are gases highly compressible?
questions.explanation: Large spaces between gas particles allow compression.

questions.question: Which of the following shows the role of dissolved oxygen?
questions.explanation: Aquatic organisms use dissolved oxygen for respiration.

questions.question: What happens when gas pressure increases in a container?
questions.explanation: Higher pressure compresses gases increasing density.

questions.question: Which experiment requires both mass and volume measurements?
questions.explanation: Density is calculated using both mass and volume.

questions.question: Which of the following increases the rate of dissolving a solid in liquid?
questions.explanation: Greater surface area allows more contact between particles.

questions.question: Why does density usually decrease when substances are heated?
questions.explanation: Heating causes expansion increasing volume.

questions.question: Which example best represents a solution used in everyday life?
questions.explanation: Salt water is a true solution.

questions.question: Why are solutions important in medicine preparation?

options.B: They ensure uniform mixing of chemicals

questions.explanation: Solutions allow medicines to mix uniformly ensuring correct dosage.
